rulings.Case Title: THOMAS E SINKIEWICZ VS. SKYLINE 83 BATAVIA INVESTORS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 18, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT WILLIAM SCHOFIELD'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is continued to September 24, 2020. On July 28, 2020 the court requested a complete set of the courtesy copies to be delivered by the moving party. The court did not receive a copy of the opposition or a copy of the operative pleading (SFLR 2.7B) as requested. Courtesy copies of opposition and the First Amended Complaint to be provided by September 11, 2020 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. KATHLEEN GILBERT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 18, 2020 line 4. DEFENDANT KATHLEEN GILBERT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED, subject to opposition. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ABIGAIL BLOOMKING VS. JAMIL MALIK BEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 18, 2020 line 7. DEFENDANT JAMIL BEY MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail addresses provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Quash is DENIED. = (501/CFH)
